step_by_step_reasoning: We trace the architecture from the transcript and visual layout. The system serves public users, content editors (newsroom), and software developers. Public users request pages which go through an Origin Proxy on ECS. Static resources are served from one S3 bucket, and pre-rendered pages from another S3 bucket. If cache is missed, request goes to a rendering Lambda function. Lambda queries templates in DynamoDB (managed by Newsroom) and pulls content via an ECS Content Proxy. This proxy caches raw CMS JSON data in ElastiCache with a TTL, retrieving from external CMS/APIs when needed. Developers upload web rendering JavaScript directly to Lambda.

graph.name: Arc Publishing Web Rendering Platform
graph.notes: Highly available multi-tier rendering architecture supporting 5-10% dynamic cache misses, leveraging ElastiCache for raw content API cache and S3 for pre-rendered pages.

nodes.name: Public Readers
nodes.notes: Readers accessing WashingtonPost.com or client sites.

nodes.name: Web Developers
nodes.notes: Deploys code updates to the Lambda rendering engine.

nodes.name: Newsroom Editors
nodes.notes: Creates page layouts via Page Builder Admin.

nodes.name: ECS Origin Proxy
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Handles traffic routing to static, cached, or dynamic targets.

nodes.name: S3 Static Resources
nodes.notes: Stores assets like JS and CSS files.

nodes.name: S3 Pre-Render Content
nodes.notes: Stores pre-rendered HTML files for fast load times.

nodes.name: Lambda Page Renderer
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Dynamically constructs web pages on-demand using JavaScript engine.

nodes.name: DynamoDB Templates
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores page builder web templates.

nodes.name: ECS Content Proxy
nodes.notes: Bridges internal rendering with external or internal CMS APIs.

nodes.name: ElastiCache Redis
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Caches raw JSON story/media payloads with active TTL.

nodes.name: CMS & APIs
nodes.notes: External content management APIs serving raw data (stories, videos).

edges.notes: Forwards to Lambda on cache miss.
Attempts to fetch raw content from cache.
Fetches raw API content on cache miss.
Saves content payload to ElastiCache with a TTL.
Modifies or saves site layout templates.
